input.prompt: Configure my statusLine to show the following information: current directory, git branch, PR number, model name, effort level, context usage, session cost limit, and weekly cost limit. Please set up the statusLine configuration in Claude Code settings.

content.text:
Everything looks good. Here is a summary of what was configured.

**Files created/updated:**

- `/Users/haru/.claude/statusline-command.sh` - the status line script
- `/Users/haru/.claude/settings.json` - updated with the `statusLine` command entry

**What the status line displays (pipe-separated):**

| Field | Example output |
|---|---|
| Current directory + git branch + PR number | `ijuku-ws1  hashed-api-key-entity` |
| PR number (extracted from branch leading digits) | `(#123)` if branch starts with a number |
| Model name | `Claude Sonnet 4.6` |
| Effort level | shown when output style is not "default" |
| Context remaining | `ctx:85%` |
| 5-hour session limit used | `5h:42%` (shown only for Claude.ai subscribers after first API response) |
| 7-day weekly limit used | `7d:10%` (same condition) |

A sample line would look like:
```
ijuku-ws1  hashed-api-key-entity | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | ctx:85% | 5h:42% | 7d:10%
```

**Notes:**
- The PR number is extracted from the leading digits of the branch name (e.g., `156-fix-note-register` -> `#156`). Your current branch `hashed-api-key-entity` has no leading number, so no PR number will appear.
- Rate limit fields only appear after the first API response in a Claude.ai subscription session.
- Git commands use `GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0` to avoid lock contention.

For any further changes to the status line, please ask Claude to update it using the **statusline-setup** agent, which is the designated agent for managing this configuration.

input.description: Fix effort level display in status line
input.prompt: The statusLine is configured but the effort level is not showing. Please update the statusline script at `/Users/haru/.claude/statusline-command.sh` to always display the effort level (even when it's "default"). The effort level should always be visible in the status line output.

content.text:
The update is complete. Here is what changed in `/Users/haru/.claude/statusline-command.sh`:

**Before:** The effort variable was set from `output_style.name`, then immediately cleared if its value was `"default"`, so it was never passed to `add_part` and never appeared in the status line.

**After:** The line that cleared the value when it equalled `"default"` has been removed. The effort is now read directly with a fallback of `"default"` if the field is absent, and it is always passed to `add_part`. The status line will now always show the effort level (e.g. `default`, `high`, `Explanatory`, etc.).

Note: For further changes to this status line, please ask Claude to update it using the statusline-setup agent. You can simply describe what you want changed and it will handle the rest.

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num="#${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Effort level (always display, even when "default")
effort=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.output_style.name // "default"')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "ctx:%.0f%%" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "5h:%.0f%%" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "7d:%.0f%%" "$week_pct")
fi

# Build output parts
parts=""
add_part() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$parts" ]; then
      parts="$1"
    else
      parts="$parts | $1"
    fi
  fi
}

# Directory + branch + PR
location="$dir"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="$location  $branch"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="$location ($pr_num)"

add_part "$location"
add_part "$model"
add_part "$effort"
add_part "$context_str"
add_part "$session_str"
add_part "$week_str"

printf "%s" "$parts"

input.description: Research effort level in Claude Code
input.prompt: How does Claude Code expose the current "effort level" or "thinking budget" to the statusLine command? The statusLine setting in Claude Code settings.json allows a shell command to be run, and its output is shown in the status bar. What environment variables, JSON fields, or other mechanisms are available to the statusLine command to get the current effort/thinking level? Please check the official Claude Code documentation.

content.text:
Based on my review of the official Claude Code documentation, specifically the **statusLine documentation** at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline.md, here's what I found:

## Answer

**The statusLine command does NOT have access to the current effort level or thinking budget.** 

Looking at the comprehensive list of available JSON fields sent to your statusLine script via stdin (documented in the ["Available data" section](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline.md#available-data)), the following fields are provided:

- Model information (`model.id`, `model.display_name`)
- Workspace info (`cwd`, `workspace.current_dir`, `workspace.project_dir`)
- Cost metrics (`cost.total_cost_usd`, `cost.total_duration_ms`)
- Context window usage (`context_window.used_percentage`, `context_window.current_usage`)
- Rate limits (for Claude.ai subscribers)
- Session info (`session_id`, `session_name`)
- Git worktree details (when applicable)
- Output style and vim mode

**Notably absent:**
- Effort level (which controls how much thinking/processing Claude uses)
- Extended thinking budget or spending
- Any thinking-related metrics

The `effortLevel` setting you have in your settings.json (`"effortLevel": "low"`) is a configuration option that affects Claude's behavior globally, but this value is not exposed to the statusLine command's JSON input.

If you wanted to display your effort level in the status line, you would need to either:
1. Hard-code it into your statusLine script based on what you manually set
2. Parse the settings.json file directly from your script (though this wouldn't reflect dynamic changes)
3. Use a separate mechanism to track and expose it

Would you like help modifying your statusLine script to incorporate the effort level information from your settings?

Based on my review of the official Claude Code documentation, specifically the **statusLine documentation** at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline.md, here's what I found:

## Answer

**The statusLine command does NOT have access to the current effort level or thinking budget.** 

Looking at the comprehensive list of available JSON fields sent to your statusLine script via stdin (documented in the ["Available data" section](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline.md#available-data)), the following fields are provided:

- Model information (`model.id`, `model.display_name`)
- Workspace info (`cwd`, `workspace.current_dir`, `workspace.project_dir`)
- Cost metrics (`cost.total_cost_usd`, `cost.total_duration_ms`)
- Context window usage (`context_window.used_percentage`, `context_window.current_usage`)
- Rate limits (for Claude.ai subscribers)
- Session info (`session_id`, `session_name`)
- Git worktree details (when applicable)
- Output style and vim mode

**Notably absent:**
- Effort level (which controls how much thinking/processing Claude uses)
- Extended thinking budget or spending
- Any thinking-related metrics

The `effortLevel` setting you have in your settings.json (`"effortLevel": "low"`) is a configuration option that affects Claude's behavior globally, but this value is not exposed to the statusLine command's JSON input.

If you wanted to display your effort level in the status line, you would need to either:
1. Hard-code it into your statusLine script based on what you manually set
2. Parse the settings.json file directly from your script (though this wouldn't reflect dynamic changes)
3. Use a separate mechanism to track and expose it

Would you like help modifying your statusLine script to incorporate the effort level information from your settings?

調査結果、**effort レベルはstatusLineのJSON入力に含まれていません**。

現在のスクリプトの `.output_style.name` は effort とは別の概念で、常に `default` になっています。

**利用可能な回避策：**

1. `settings.json` を直接読み込む（`effortLevel` の設定値を表示）
2. スクリプトにハードコード

`settings.json` を読み込む方法であれば、設定を変更した際に反映されます。対応しましょうか？

#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num="#${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Effort level (always display, even when "default")
effort=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.output_style.name // "default"')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "ctx:%.0f%%" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "5h:%.0f%%" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "7d:%.0f%%" "$week_pct")
fi

# Build output parts
parts=""
add_part() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$parts" ]; then
      parts="$1"
    else
      parts="$parts | $1"
    fi
  fi
}

# Directory + branch + PR
location="$dir"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="$location  $branch"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="$location ($pr_num)"

add_part "$location"
add_part "$model"
add_part "$effort"
add_part "$context_str"
add_part "$session_str"
add_part "$week_str"

printf "%s" "$parts"

#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num="#${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "ctx:%.0f%%" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "5h:%.0f%%" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "7d:%.0f%%" "$week_pct")
fi

# Build output parts
parts=""
add_part() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$parts" ]; then
      parts="$1"
    else
      parts="$parts | $1"
    fi
  fi
}

# Directory + branch + PR
location="$dir"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="$location  $branch"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="$location ($pr_num)"

add_part "$location"
add_part "$model"
add_part "$context_str"
add_part "$session_str"
add_part "$week_str"

printf "%s" "$parts"

input.content:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "󰓅 ctx:%.0f%%" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "󱑍 5h:%.0f%%" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "󰃭 7d:%.0f%%" "$week_pct")
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location=" $dir"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="$location   $branch"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="$location ($pr_num)"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 " $model"
add2 "$context_str"
add2 "$session_str"
add2 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

更新しました。表示イメージ：

```
 ijuku-ws1    hashed-api-key-entity
  Claude Sonnet 4.6   󰓅 ctx:85%   󱑍 5h:42%   󰃭 7d:10%
```

使用アイコン：
- `` フォルダ（ディレクトリ）
- `` git ブランチ
- `` モデル（ロボット）
- `󰓅` コンテキスト
- `󱑍` 時計（5時間）
- `󰃭` カレンダー（7日）

Nerd Font がインストールされていないと文字化けするのでご注意ください。

すでにスクリプトに含まれています！

- `` → フォルダ（ディレクトリ名の前）

表示例：
```
 ijuku-ws1    hashed-api-key-entity

```

ターミナルでアイコンが表示されない場合は、Nerd Font（例：`JetBrainsMono Nerd Font`）をターミナルのフォントに設定してください。

#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "󰓅 ctx:%.0f%%" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "󱑍 5h:%.0f%%" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "󰃭 7d:%.0f%%" "$week_pct")
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location=" $dir"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="$location   $branch"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="$location ($pr_num)"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 " $model"
add2 "$context_str"
add2 "$session_str"
add2 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

#!/bin/sh

input=$(cat)

ICON_ROBOT=$(printf '\uf06d1')   # nf-md-robot (model)
ICON_CTX=$(printf '\uf4bc')      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(printf '\uf017')    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(printf '\uf073')      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "%s ctx:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CTX" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "%s 5h:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"
add2 "$session_str"
add2 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

content.text:
`printf '\uXXXX'` 形式でアイコンを実行時に生成するよう変更しました。

使用アイコン：
| 用途 | コード | アイコン名 |
|---|---|---|
| フォルダ | `\uf07b` | nf-fa-folder |
| ブランチ | `\ue0a0` | nf-pl-branch |
| モデル | `\uf06d1` | nf-md-robot |
| コンテキスト | `\uf4bc` | nf-fa-microchip |
| 5h制限 | `\uf017` | nf-fa-clock |
| 7d制限 | `\uf073` | nf-fa-calendar |

表示を確認してみてください。もし特定のアイコンだけ出ない場合は、別のコードポイントに差し替えます。

hookInfos.command: say "Claude Code の処理が完了しました"
entire hooks claude-code stop

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons (ANSI-C quoting)
ICON_FOLDER=$'\uf07b'   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$'\ue0a0'   # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$'\uf06d1'   # nf-md-robot (model)
ICON_CTX=$'\uf4bc'      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$'\uf017'    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$'\uf073'      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "%s ctx:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CTX" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "%s 5h:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"
add2 "$session_str"
add2 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

input.prompt: What is the exact JSON field name for the session reset time in Claude Code's statusLine command input? Looking for something like rate_limits.five_hour.reset_at or similar. Please check the statusLine documentation.

content.text:
Perfect! I found the exact JSON field names for the session reset time in Claude Code's statusLine command input.

Based on the official Claude Code statusLine documentation, the exact JSON field names for session reset time are:

- **`rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at`** - Unix epoch seconds when the 5-hour rate limit window resets
- **`rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at`** - Unix epoch seconds when the 7-day rate limit window resets

These fields are part of the `rate_limits` object that Claude Code sends to your statusLine script via stdin. Both fields contain Unix epoch timestamps (seconds since January 1, 1970) indicating when the respective rate limit windows reset.

Note that the `rate_limits` field is only present for Claude.ai subscribers (Pro/Max) after the first API response in the session. You should handle the absence of these fields gracefully in your script using fallbacks like `// empty` in jq.

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)   # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "%s ctx:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CTX" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(printf "%s 5h:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"
add2 "$session_str"
add2 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)   # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage (remaining %)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(printf "%s ctx:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CTX" "$remaining")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str=$(printf "%s 5h:%.0f%% (↺%s)" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct" "$reset_time")
  else
    session_str=$(printf "%s 5h:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%% (↺%s)" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct" "$reset_date")
  else
    week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
add3() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line3" ]; then line3="$1"; else line3="$line3  $1"; fi
  fi
}

add3 "$session_str"
add3 "$week_str"

# Output
if [ -n "$line3" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2" "$line3"
elif [ -n "$line2" ]; then
  printf "%s\n%s" "$line1" "$line2"
else
  printf "%s" "$line1"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)   # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
print(f'{bar} {used:.0f}%', end='')
")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  session_str=$(python3 -c "
import datetime, sys
pct = $five_pct
bar_len = 10
filled = round(pct * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
reset_ts = '$five_reset'
if reset_ts:
    jst = datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9))
    reset_time = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(int(reset_ts), tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M JST')
    print(f'{pct:.0f}% {bar} by {reset_time}', end='')
else:
    print(f'{pct:.0f}% {bar}', end='')
")
  session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${session_str}"
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%% (↺%s)" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct" "$reset_date")
  else
    week_str=$(printf "%s 7d:%.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"

# --- Line 3: 5h limit ---
line3="$session_str"

# --- Line 4: 7d limit ---
line4="$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
[ -n "$line4" ] && output="$output\n$line4"
printf "%b" "$output"

session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%% by %s" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct" "$reset_time")
  else
    session_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%% by %s" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct" "$reset_date")
  else
    week_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"

# --- Line 3: 5h limit ---
line3="$session_str"

# --- Line 4: 7d limit ---
line4="$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
[ -n "$line4" ] && output="$output\n$line4"
printf "%b" "$output"

message.content: by ってまで的なニュアンスで使っているんだけどおかしくない？

content.text:
ご指摘の通りです。「by」は英語で「〜までに」という締め切りのニュアンスがありますが、リセット時刻に使うと「リセットされるまで」という意味で自然に通じます。ただ、より直感的にするなら `resets at` や `↺` などの方が明確かもしれません。

どれが好みですか？

1. `↺ 09:00` （リセットアイコン）
2. `resets 09:00`
3. `reset: 09:00`
4. `→ 09:00`
5. このまま `by 09:00`

input.prompt: Does Claude Code's statusLine support ANSI color escape codes in the output? Can the statusLine command output colored text using ANSI escape sequences like \033[32m etc?

content.text:
Yes, Claude Code's statusLine **does support ANSI color escape codes**. According to the official documentation:

## ANSI Color Support

From the statusLine documentation under "What your script can output":

> **Colors**: use [ANSI escape codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANSI_escape_code#Colors) like `\033[32m` for green (terminal must support them).

The documentation explicitly mentions that you can use escape sequences like `\033[32m` (green), and provides real examples throughout the docs that demonstrate colored output.

## Example Usage

Here's a real example from the "Git status with colors" section in the docs that shows ANSI colors in action:

```bash
GREEN='\033[32m'
YELLOW='\033[33m'
RESET='\033[0m'

echo -e "[$MODEL] 📁 ${DIR##*/} | 🌿 $BRANCH ${GREEN}+${STAGED}${RESET}${YELLOW}~${MODIFIED}${RESET}"
```

And in the "Display multiple lines" example, there's this with threshold-based coloring:

```bash
CYAN='\033[36m'; GREEN='\033[32m'; YELLOW='\033[33m'; RED='\033[31m'; RESET='\033[0m'
# ... then using the colors
echo -e "${CYAN}[$MODEL]${RESET} 📁 ${DIR##*/}$BRANCH"
echo -e "${BAR_COLOR}${BAR}${RESET} ${PCT}% | ${YELLOW}${COST_FMT}${RESET} | ⏱️ ${MINS}m ${SECS}s"
```

## Requirements

The one caveat is: **your terminal must support ANSI color codes** (most modern terminals do). The documentation also mentions you can use OSC 8 escape sequences for clickable links if your terminal supports them (iTerm2, Kitty, WezTerm).

Your `/Users/haru/.claude/statusline-command.sh` is already configured in your settings, so you can add ANSI escape codes directly to that script for colored output.

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)   # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
print(f'{bar} {used:.0f}%', end='')
")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%% by %s" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct" "$reset_time")
  else
    session_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%% by %s" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct" "$reset_date")
  else
    week_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%%" "$ICON_CAL" "$week_pct")
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
line1=""
add1() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line1" ]; then line1="$1"; else line1="$line1  $1"; fi
  fi
}

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# ANSI colors
CYAN='\033[36m'
GREEN='\033[32m'
YELLOW='\033[33m'
RED='\033[31m'
BLUE='\033[34m'
MAGENTA='\033[35m'
DIM='\033[2m'
RESET='\033[0m'

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\033[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\033[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\033[32m'   # green
reset = '\033[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=$1
  if awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 80)}"; then echo -e "$RED"
  elif awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 50)}"; then echo -e "$YELLOW"
  else echo -e "$GREEN"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2="${MAGENTA}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\033[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\033[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\033[32m'   # green
reset = '\033[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=$1
  if awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 80)}"; then echo -e "$RED"
  elif awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 50)}"; then echo -e "$YELLOW"
  else echo -e "$GREEN"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2="${MAGENTA}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)   # nf-pl-branch

ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
print(f'{bar} {used:.0f}%', end='')
")
fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then

  else
    session_str=$(printf "%s %.0f%%" "$ICON_CLOCK" "$five_pct")
  fi

  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then

  fi

location="${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}"

[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} (${pr_num})"
add1 "$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2=""
add2() {
  if [ -n "$1" ]; then
    if [ -z "$line2" ]; then line2="$1"; else line2="$line2  $1"; fi
  fi
}

[ -n "$model" ] && add2 "${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}"
add2 "$context_str"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

branch=""

  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\033[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\033[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\033[32m'   # green
reset = '\033[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=$1
  if awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 80)}"; then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif awk "BEGIN{exit !($pct >= 50)}"; then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$GREEN"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""

  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2="${MAGENTA}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# ANSI colors
CYAN=$'\033[36m'
GREEN=$'\033[32m'
YELLOW=$'\033[33m'
RED=$'\033[31m'
BLUE=$'\033[34m'
MAGENTA=$'\033[35m'
DIM=$'\033[2m'
RESET=$'\033[0m'

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\x1b[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\x1b[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\x1b[34m'   # blue
reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer
  if (( pct >= 80 )); then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$BLUE"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2="${MAGENTA}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\x1b[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\x1b[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\x1b[92m'   # bright green
reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer
  if (( pct >= 80 )); then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$GREEN"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
line2="${MAGENTA}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

RESET=$'\033[0m'

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\x1b[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\x1b[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\x1b[2m'    # dim
reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer
  if (( pct >= 80 )); then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$DIM"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
model=$(echo "$model" | sed 's/ ([0-9]*[kKmMbB] context)/ (\1)/g; s/ context)/)/g; s/([0-9]*[kKmMbB] context)/(\1)/g')
case "$model" in
  *Opus*)   model_color="$YELLOW" ;;
  *Haiku*)  model_color="$RED" ;;
  *)        model_color="$DIM" ;;  # Sonnet and others
esac
line2="${model_color}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

content.text: Sonnet は `model_color` が DIM なので、DIM 属性が残ったまま RED が適用されて暗くなっています。`(1M)` の前に一旦 RESET してから RED を適用します。

reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer
  if (( pct >= 80 )); then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$DIM"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
model=$(echo "$model" | sed 's/ context)/)/g')
case "$model" in
  *Opus*)   model_color="$YELLOW" ;;
  *Haiku*)  model_color="$RED" ;;
  *)        model_color="$DIM" ;;  # Sonnet and others
esac
model=$(echo "$model" | sed "s/(\([0-9]*[kKmMbB]\))/${RED}(\1)${model_color}/g")
line2="${model_color}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# ANSI colors
CYAN=$'\033[36m'
GREEN=$'\033[92m'
YELLOW=$'\033[33m'
RED=$'\033[31m'
BLUE=$'\033[34m'
MAGENTA=$'\033[35m'
DIM=$'\033[2m'
RESET=$'\033[0m'

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)
if used >= 80:
    color = '\x1b[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\x1b[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\x1b[2m'    # dim
reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

# Helper: usage color
_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer
  if (( pct >= 80 )); then printf '%s' "$RED"
  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$DIM"
  fi
}

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
model=$(echo "$model" | sed 's/ context)/)/g')
case "$model" in
  *Opus*)   model_color="$YELLOW" ;;
  *Haiku*)  model_color="$RED" ;;
  *)        model_color="$DIM" ;;  # Sonnet and others
esac
model=$(echo "$model" | sed "s/(\([0-9]*[kKmMbB]\))/${RESET}${RED}(\1)${RESET}${model_color}/g")
line2="${model_color}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"

#!/bin/bash
# Claude Code status line script

input=$(cat)

# ANSI colors
CYAN=$'\033[36m'
GREEN=$'\033[92m'
YELLOW=$'\033[33m'
RED=$'\033[31m'
BLUE=$'\033[34m'
MAGENTA=$'\033[35m'
DIM=$'\033[2m'
RESET=$'\033[0m'

# Nerd Font icons via python3 (ensures correct UTF-8 encoding)
_icon() { python3 -c "import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(chr($1).encode('utf-8'))"; }
ICON_FOLDER=$(_icon 0xf07b)   # nf-fa-folder
ICON_BRANCH=$(_icon 0xF062C)  # nf-pl-branch
ICON_ROBOT=$(_icon 0xF06A9)   # nf-fa-brain (model)
ICON_CTX=$(_icon 0xf4bc)      # nf-fa-microchip (context)
ICON_CLOCK=$(_icon 0xf017)    # nf-fa-clock_o (5h limit)
ICON_CAL=$(_icon 0xf073)      # nf-fa-calendar (7d limit)

# Current directory (basename)
cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
dir=$(basename "$cwd")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
branch=""
if [ -n "$cwd" ] && [ -d "$cwd/.git" ] || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0 git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
fi

# PR number from branch name (extract leading digits, e.g. "123-fix-bug" -> "#123")
pr_num=""
if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
  pr_num=$(echo "$branch" | grep -oE '^[0-9]+' | head -1)
  [ -n "$pr_num" ] && pr_num=" #${pr_num}"
fi

# Model name
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

# Context usage progress bar (color based on usage)
remaining=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
context_str=""
if [ -n "$remaining" ]; then
  context_str=$(python3 -c "
used = 100 - $remaining
bar_len = 10
filled = round(used * bar_len / 100)
bar = '\u2588' * filled + '\u2591' * (bar_len - filled)

    color = '\x1b[31m'   # red
elif used >= 50:
    color = '\x1b[33m'   # yellow
else:
    color = '\x1b[2m'    # dim
reset = '\x1b[0m'
print(f'{color}{bar} {used:.0f}%{reset}', end='')
")
fi

_usage_color() {
  local pct=${1%.*}  # truncate to integer

  elif (( pct >= 50 )); then printf '%s' "$YELLOW"
  else printf '%s' "$DIM"
  fi

# Session (5-hour) cost limit
five_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage // empty')
five_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.five_hour.resets_at // empty')
session_str=""
if [ -n "$five_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$five_pct")
  reset_time=""
  [ -n "$five_reset" ] && reset_time=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($five_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_time" ]; then
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_time}${RESET}"
  else
    session_str="${ICON_CLOCK} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$five_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# Weekly (7-day) cost limit
week_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.used_percentage // empty')
week_reset=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.rate_limits.seven_day.resets_at // empty')
week_str=""
if [ -n "$week_pct" ]; then
  col=$(_usage_color "$week_pct")
  reset_date=""
  [ -n "$week_reset" ] && reset_date=$(python3 -c "import datetime; jst=datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=9)); print(datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp($week_reset, tz=jst).strftime('%m/%d %H:%M'))")
  if [ -n "$reset_date" ]; then
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET} ${DIM}by ${reset_date}${RESET}"
  else
    week_str="${ICON_CAL} ${col}$(printf "%.0f%%" "$week_pct")${RESET}"
  fi
fi

# --- Line 1: location info ---
location="${CYAN}${ICON_FOLDER} ${dir}${RESET}"
[ -n "$branch" ] && location="${location}  ${GREEN}${ICON_BRANCH} ${branch}${RESET}"
[ -n "$pr_num" ] && location="${location} ${DIM}(${pr_num})${RESET}"
line1="$location"

# --- Line 2: AI info ---
model=$(echo "$model" | sed 's/ context)/)/g; s/ [0-9]*\.[0-9]*//g')
case "$model" in
  *Opus*)   model_color="$YELLOW" ;;
  *Haiku*)  model_color="$RED" ;;
  *)        model_color="$DIM" ;;  # Sonnet and others
esac
model=$(echo "$model" | sed "s/(\([0-9]*[kKmMbB]\))/${RESET}${RED}(\1)${RESET}${model_color}/g")

model_padded=$(python3 -c "
import re
text = '$model'
visible = re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*m', '', text)
target = len('Sonnet 4.6 (1M)')
pad = max(0, target - len(visible))
print(' ' * pad + text, end='')
")
line2="${model_color}${ICON_ROBOT} ${model_padded}${RESET}"
[ -n "$context_str" ] && line2="${line2}  ${context_str}"

# --- Line 3: limit info ---
line3=""
[ -n "$session_str" ] && line3="$session_str"
[ -n "$week_str" ] && line3="${line3:+$line3  }$week_str"

# Output
output="$line1"
[ -n "$line2" ] && output="$output\n$line2"
[ -n "$line3" ] && output="$output\n$line3"
printf "%b" "$output"
